rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION/DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Friday, May-01-2026, LINE 1. RESPONDENT SEPHORA USA, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration. The court sua sponte grants reconsideration and vacates its order of March 13, 2026 granting petitioner's motion to compel arbitration. Sephora has leave to file a supplemental brief of no more than five pages concerning the issues of petitioner's actual or constructive notice of any relevant Beauty Insider Terms and Conditions discussed in the court's now-vacated March 13 order. Sephora shall file its brief by May 7, 2026. Petitioner may file a supplemental brief in response of no more than five pages by May 13, 2026. The petition is set for further hearing on May 19, 2026. Sephora's motion for reconsideration is denied as moot. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: MAHER MEMARZADEH VS. AMGUARD INSURANCE COMPANY D/B/A BERKSHIRE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted, Motion For Evidentiary Sanction Or In The Alternative To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Requests For Admission (Set One)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION/DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Friday, May-01-2026, LINE 30. PLAINTIFF MAHER MEMARZADEH'S Motion To Deem Requests For Admission Admitted, Motion For Evidentiary Sanction Or In The Alternative To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories (Set One), Special Interrogatories (Set One), And Requests For Admission (Set One). Plaintiff Maher Memarzadeh's discovery motion is granted in part. Plaintiff shows that defendant AMGUARD Insurance Company D/B/A Berkshire Hathaway Guard Insurance Companies has failed to respond to his form interrogatories, set one; special interrogatories, set one; and requests for admission, set one. Plaintiff seeks evidentiary sanctions, but this is the first discovery motion the court has considered on its merits, and evidentiary sanctions are not appropriate as a first discovery order. The court orders defendant to respond to the outstanding interrogatories within three weeks of this order. The court orders that all matters specified in the requests for admission attached to the Memarzadeh Declaration at pdf pages 94-130 are deemed admitted by defendant. Defendant shall pay $406.50 to plaintiff in sanctions within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: KAMLESH BANGA VS. LAWRENCE R. LUSTIG M.D. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THIS MATTER SHOULD NOT BE STAYED
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION/DISCOVERY CALENDAR for Friday, May-01-2026, LINE 33. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THIS MATTER SHOULD NOT BE STAYED. This court previously issued an order to show cause why this case should not be stayed until final disposition of Banga v. UCSF Medical Center, CGC-16-549780 ("Banga I"), which has factual overlap with this case. Plaintiff filed a First Amended Complaint on April 16, 2026. In light of Plaintiff's amended complaint, the court continues the hearing on its order to show cause to June 1, 2026. Plaintiff may file a supplemental brief of no more than 15 pages in opposition to a stay order by May 15, 2026. Defendants may file a supplemental brief of no more than 15 pages by May 22, 2026. Because the purpose of any stay would be to serve judicial efficiency by holding this case in abeyance while the Court of Appeal considers potentially overlapping issues in Banga I, and to avoid wasted litigation efforts pending final resolution of Banga I, the court extends Defendants' time to respond to the First Amended Complaint to June 15, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number.
